entry_symptom: BFD session cycling between Up and Down states repeatedly

commands.command: docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show bfd peers'

nodes.question: How frequently is the BFD session flapping (check syslog for BFD state transitions)?
Is the control plane CPU overloaded? (bfdd is missing keepalives due to scheduling starvation)

nodes.action: bfdd is a software process sharing CPU with other control-plane daemons. Under high load it cannot send or process BFD control packets within the negotiated detect interval, causing the peer to declare the session Down. Remediation: (1) Identify the CPU consumer: ps aux --sort=-%cpu | head -10. (2) Reduce BFD aggressiveness: increase tx-interval (e.g. 300ms -> 1000ms) and/or increase detect-multiplier (3 -> 5) to give more tolerance. (3) If available on this platform, check whether hardware-offloaded BFD is supported (avoids control-plane scheduling entirely). (4) Consider isolating the BFD container or adjusting process priorities with nice/chrt if the offending process is low-priority. Verify recovery: watch 'show bfd peers' until state stays Up.

commands.command: docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show bfd peers counters'
docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show running-config' | grep -A5 'bfd'

nodes.finding: CPU starvation causing BFD keepalive misses

commands.command: docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show bfd peers'
docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show bfd peers json'

nodes.question: Are the BFD timers asymmetric between the two peers (tx-interval, rx-interval, or detect-multiplier mismatch)?

nodes.action: BFD negotiates the actual transmit interval as max(local-tx, remote-rx) and the detect timeout as negotiated-interval * detect-multiplier. If one peer has a very short detect-multiplier and a long tx-interval on the other side, the detection time becomes shorter than the transmission period and sessions flip. Fix: (1) Align tx-interval and detect-multiplier symmetrically on both peers. Recommended starting values: tx-interval 300, rx-interval 300, detect-multiplier 3 (900ms detection). (2) Apply on the SONiC side via: config bfd peer <peer_ip> interval <ms> min-rx <ms> multiplier <n>. (3) Verify negotiated values converge: docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show bfd peers json' -- look at 'negotiated-transmission-interval' and 'remote-*' fields.

commands.command: docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show bfd peers json'

nodes.finding: BFD timer mismatch between peers causing detect timeout

nodes.question: Is there packet loss on the link (check interface error counters, CRC errors, or input drops)?

nodes.action: BFD is sensitive to packet loss -- even a single dropped control packet counts toward the detect-multiplier threshold. CRC errors, optical Rx power issues, or cable problems manifest as intermittent BFD drops before they trip interface errors. Remediation: (1) Check optical Rx/Tx power: show interfaces transceiver dom <port>. (2) Clean or replace SFP/DAC cable if Rx power is near the threshold. (3) Short-term mitigation: increase detect-multiplier to tolerate isolated drops (e.g. multiplier 5 instead of 3). (4) If errors persist, replace the physical medium. Verify: show interfaces counters errors -- CRC counter should stop incrementing after fix.
nodes.finding: Physical link quality degradation causing BFD packet loss

commands.command: docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show bfd peers json' | python3 -m json.tool | grep -i echo
docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show running-config' | grep -A10 'bfd peer'

nodes.question: Is BFD echo mode enabled on this session?

nodes.action: Two common causes reach this leaf: (1) CoPP rate-limiting: BFD packets are punted to the CPU and policed by the COPP_TABLE entry for BFD. If the policer rate is too low, BFD packets are dropped silently. Check: redis-cli -n 4 HGETALL 'COPP_TABLE|copp-system-bfd'. If cir/cbs is very small, increase it. Also check hardware counters: show dropcounters counts. (2) Multi-hop BFD reachability: if a routing change briefly removes the path to the BFD peer, the session drops even with healthy timers. Check: show ip route <peer_ip> and correlate BFD flap timestamps with route change timestamps in syslog. For multi-hop BFD, consider using a dedicated static route with a higher admin distance as a backup to prevent route flaps from triggering BFD drops.

commands.command: docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show bfd peers counters'

nodes.finding: CoPP policer rate-limiting BFD control packets or routing change disrupting multi-hop BFD reachability

nodes.action: Echo mode requires the remote device to loop BFD echo packets back to the originator without processing them. Some platforms (particularly non-SONiC peers or older ASICs) do not correctly loop echo packets, causing the echo detect timer to fire and the session to go Down. Remediation: (1) Disable echo mode on SONiC: configure the BFD peer without echo-mode. In FRR config: 'no echo-mode' under the BFD peer. (2) Alternatively, verify the peer platform supports echo loopback per its vendor documentation. (3) After disabling echo mode, the session will use asynchronous mode only. Verify: docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show bfd peers json' -- 'echo-mode' field should be false and session should stay Up.

commands.command: docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show bfd peers json'
docker exec bgp vtysh -c 'show running-config' | grep -A15 'bfd peer'

nodes.finding: BFD echo mode incompatibility -- peer not looping back echo packets correctly
